claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital/residential accommodation for 15 weeks (>12) and claimant is not regularly spending money on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child in hospital/residential accommodation for 15 weeks (>12) and claimant is not regularly spending money on child
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital/residential accommodation for 15 weeks (>12) and claimant is not regularly spending money on child
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for? If yes, how many children are you asking about, and what are their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My son Alex is 8 years old, and I'm hoping to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask: Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! And do you live with Alex, or does he live with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or another immigration status?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! One final question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex (such as the other parent or another family member)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- **Age requirement met**: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16
- **Responsibility**: You live with Alex, which means you're responsible for him
- **Residency**: You're a UK citizen living in the UK
- **No conflicting claim**: No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can proceed with your application for Child Benefit for Alex. If you need information on how to claim, you can visit the government website or contact the Child Benefit Office for assistance.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. You are responsible for Alex as you live with him in the UK. You are a UK citizen living in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict with another claimant. You meet all the eligibility criteria for claiming Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with their 8-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex as they live together, Alex is under 16 years old, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. You are responsible for Alex as you live with him in the UK. You are a UK citizen living in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict with another claimant. You meet all the eligibility criteria for claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with their 8-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex as they live together, Alex is under 16 years old, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.
